howItWorks.intro: The calculator adds up every category of vehicle expense and divides by miles driven to find your true per-mile cost.
rules: Fuel cost per mile = fuel price ÷ MPG
Depreciation per mile = (purchase price − resale value) ÷ (years × annual miles)
Fixed costs per mile = (insurance + maintenance + registration) ÷ annual miles
Total cost per mile = fuel + depreciation + fixed costs
howItWorks.outro: The IRS standard mileage rate for 2024 is $0.67/mile. Most personal vehicles cost between $0.40 and $0.80 per mile when all expenses are included.

faq.question: What is a good cost per mile for a car?
faq.answer: The average cost per mile is about $0.50-$0.70 for a typical sedan. Economy cars can be as low as $0.35/mile, while trucks and SUVs often exceed $0.75/mile. The IRS standard mileage rate of $0.67 is a reasonable benchmark.

faq.question: Should I include depreciation in cost per mile?
faq.answer: Yes. Depreciation is typically the largest single cost of vehicle ownership, especially in the first 5 years. A $35,000 car that's worth $20,000 after 5 years has lost $3,000/year to depreciation alone.

faq.question: How does mileage affect cost per mile?
faq.answer: Higher annual mileage spreads fixed costs (insurance, registration) over more miles, reducing cost per mile for those categories. However, higher mileage increases maintenance costs and depreciation rate.

faq.question: What costs are not included here?
faq.answer: This calculator covers the major costs. Additional expenses like parking, tolls, car washes, loan interest, and traffic tickets could add $0.02-$0.10 per mile depending on your situation.

faq.question: Is the Cost Per Mile Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Cost Per Mile Calculator?
sections.body: The Cost Per Mile Calculator is a free online tool designed for car owners and enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the automotive care and maintenance space. By entering your annual miles driven, fuel price, fuel economy, you get instant results including total cost per mile, annual total cost, fuel cost/mile. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total cost per mile right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In automotive care and maintenance, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by car owners and enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to maintain your vehicle with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your annual miles driven and need to find the right total cost per mile. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Annual Miles Driven
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your annual miles driven — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: fuel price, fuel economy, purchase price, current / resale value, years of ownership, annual insurance, annual maintenance, annual registration & fees. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total cost per mile, annual total cost, fuel cost/mile. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your annual miles driven before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total cost per mile and annual total cost — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to maintain your vehicle.
If you're unsure about your annual registration & fees, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
